reason: Calcium chelates levothyroxine in the gut, forming insoluble complexes that dramatically reduce thyroid hormone absorption, leading to hypothyroidism.
recommendation: Take levothyroxine on empty stomach 30–60 min before breakfast. Take calcium at lunch or evening. Never co-administer.

reason: Ferrous iron forms insoluble complexes with levothyroxine, reducing its absorption by up to 60–80% when taken together.
recommendation: Always separate levothyroxine and iron by at least 4 hours. Administer levothyroxine at dawn, iron at midday or evening.

reason: Tannins and polyphenols in tea and coffee bind dietary and supplemental iron, inhibiting non-haem iron absorption by 60–90%.
recommendation: Take iron on empty stomach or 1–2 hours away from tea, coffee, or any tannin beverage. Vitamin C (orange juice) co-administration improves absorption.
source_name: WHO — Iron Deficiency Anaemia Assessment, Prevention and Control

reason: Calcium competes with iron for intestinal absorption via the same divalent metal transporter (DMT-1). Co-administration significantly reduces iron uptake.
recommendation: Never take iron and calcium together. Stagger by at least 2 hours. Preferably take iron in the morning and calcium with evening meal.
source_name: WHO — Iron Deficiency Anaemia Assessment, Prevention and Control

reason: PPIs are prodrugs that require acid to activate the canalicular proton pump. Taking with or after food reduces efficacy by up to 50% because active pumps are needed for activation.
recommendation: Always take pantoprazole or omeprazole 30–60 minutes before the first meal of the day on an empty stomach.

medicine_b_or_food: Antacids containing aluminium or magnesium (Digene / Gelusil)
reason: Aluminium- and magnesium-containing antacids reduce peak azithromycin plasma concentration (Cmax) by approximately 24%, reducing antimicrobial effect.
recommendation: Take azithromycin at least 2 hours before, or 2 hours after, antacid intake. Azithromycin is best taken on an empty stomach.

reason: Calcium and divalent cations (Ca2+, Mg2+, Al3+, Fe2+) chelate fluoroquinolones in the gut, forming poorly absorbed complexes. This can reduce ciprofloxacin bioavailability by 30–60%.
recommendation: Do not take ciprofloxacin within 2 hours of any dairy product, calcium supplement, or antacid. Take with plain water on an empty stomach.

minimum_gap: Take WITH or immediately after food
reason: Metformin causes significant GI side effects (nausea, diarrhoea, abdominal pain) when taken on an empty stomach. Food slows absorption and improves tolerability without reducing efficacy.
recommendation: Always take metformin with or immediately after meals. Starting at low dose (500 mg) and slowly titrating also helps GI tolerance.

minimum_gap: Avoid alcohol entirely during warfarin therapy
reason: Acute alcohol intake inhibits warfarin metabolism (CYP2C9), increasing INR and bleeding risk. Chronic heavy use induces CYP enzymes, reducing warfarin effect and increasing clot risk. Both extremes are dangerous.
recommendation: Avoid alcohol completely during warfarin therapy. If unavoidable, limit to 1–2 standard drinks, maintain consistent intake, and monitor INR more frequently.

medicine_b_or_food: Vitamin K-rich foods (green leafy vegetables: spinach, methi, coriander)
minimum_gap: No gap — maintain consistent daily intake rather than avoiding
reason: Warfarin acts by inhibiting vitamin K-dependent clotting factors. Large or erratic changes in vitamin K consumption significantly alter INR. Sudden increase in vitamin K reduces anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Do NOT eliminate vitamin K foods — eat consistent amounts daily. Avoid sudden large changes in intake. INR monitoring should guide dose adjustments.

reason: Cetirizine is a second-generation antihistamine with CNS sedation potential. Alcohol potentiates CNS depression, increasing drowsiness, impaired motor function, and reaction time — particularly dangerous while driving.
recommendation: Avoid alcohol while taking cetirizine, especially if driving or operating machinery. Even though cetirizine is 'less sedating', alcohol interaction remains clinically significant.

minimum_gap: 48 hours after last dose of metronidazole
reason: Metronidazole inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase, causing a disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol: severe nausea, vomiting, flushing, tachycardia, hypotension. Can be life-threatening.
recommendation: Absolutely no alcohol during metronidazole therapy AND for 48 hours after the last dose. Warn patients explicitly. Also avoid alcohol-containing mouthwashes, sauces, and cough syrups.

reason: Polyvalent cations in antacids (Al3+, Mg2+) form insoluble chelates with quinolones and reduce absorption of many oral antibiotics by altering gut pH and binding drug molecules.
recommendation: As a general rule, take any oral antibiotic at least 2 hours before, or 2 hours after, any antacid. This is most critical for fluoroquinolones (ciprofloxacin) and tetracyclines.

minimum_gap: Take ibuprofen at least 30 min AFTER aspirin, or 8+ hours before the next aspirin dose
reason: Ibuprofen competes with aspirin for COX-1 binding sites on platelets. If taken before aspirin, it blocks aspirin's irreversible platelet inhibition, negating cardioprotective antiplatelet effect.
recommendation: Patients on daily low-dose aspirin (75–150 mg) for cardioprotection should take aspirin first, wait at least 30 minutes, then take ibuprofen if needed. Preferably avoid regular ibuprofen in patients on antiplatelet aspirin.

minimum_gap: Avoid combination — use pantoprazole instead if PPI needed
reason: Omeprazole is a strong CYP2C19 inhibitor. Clopidogrel requires CYP2C19 conversion to its active form. Co-administration reduces clopidogrel's antiplatelet effect by 30–60%, increasing risk of stent thrombosis and MI.
recommendation: Use pantoprazole (weaker CYP2C19 inhibition) instead of omeprazole in patients on clopidogrel. If unavoidable, stagger timing: take clopidogrel in the morning and omeprazole at night (partial mitigation only).

minimum_gap: 30–60 minutes (bisphosphonate must be taken first, on empty stomach)
reason: Calcium (and food, or any medication) taken within 30–60 min of alendronate dramatically reduces bisphosphonate absorption from near-complete to near-zero. Must be taken with plain water only.
recommendation: Take alendronate immediately upon waking with a full glass of plain water only. Remain upright (no lying down) for 30–60 min. Take calcium (and all other medications/food) only after this window.

medicine_b_or_food: Soy-based foods / soy milk / soy supplements
reason: Dietary soy isoflavones may reduce levothyroxine absorption. High soy intake is associated with increased levothyroxine dose requirements, particularly in infants on soy formula.
recommendation: Maintain a 4-hour gap between levothyroxine and high soy intake. Patients on high-soy diets may need TSH monitoring and dose adjustment.

reason: Divalent cations in dairy products (Ca2+) form insoluble chelate complexes with tetracyclines, reducing antibiotic absorption by up to 50–80%.
recommendation: Take tetracycline/doxycycline with a full glass of water only, at least 1–2 hours before or 2–3 hours after dairy products. Never take with milk.
